ddls:
CREATE TABLE "authorities" (
  "authority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each local authority */,
  "authority_name" TEXT /* Name of the local authority */,
  "ons_code" TEXT /* ONS (Office for National Statistics) code for the local authority */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the local authority */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the local authority in square kilometers */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the local authority */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the local authority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("authority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "academic_years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each academic year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Name of the academic year (e.g., 2005/06) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the academic year */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the academic year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_counts" (
  "count_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school count record */,
  "authority_id" INTEGER /* ID of the local authority */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* ID of the academic year */,
  "school_count" INTEGER /* Number of schools in the local authority for the given academic year */,
  "student_count" INTEGER /* Number of students in the local authority for the given academic year */,
  "teacher_count" INTEGER /* Number of teachers in the local authority for the given academic year */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of school (e.g., primary, secondary, special) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("count_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_counts_authority_id FOREIGN KEY ("authority_id") REFERENCES authorities ("authority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_counts_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES academic_years ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data entry clerk, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_entry_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the data entry */,
  "count_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school count record being edited */,
  "entry_date" TEXT /* Date when the data entry was performed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., add, update, delete) */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the data entry */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the data entry */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the data entry action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entry_logs_count_id FOREIGN KEY ("count_id") REFERENCES school_counts ("count_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entry_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., trend analysis, comparative analysis) */,
  "generated_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report (e.g., text, JSON, CSV) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the file where the report is stored (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
